safest_plan.entry_basis: No price level exists. The only chart is a Commitments-of-Traders positioning page (Long vs. Short bars), not a price chart — it carries no entry zone. The text's 'value at low retest' was flagged by its own grader as 'exact level unclear from partial screenshot, estimated.'

targets.basis: No target levels are stated or drawable; 'value at high retest' is a generic placeholder with no number.

safest_plan.stop_basis: No structural level given — the source shows positioning data, not price structure, so no stop can be anchored.

stance_by_source.summary: An educational walk-through of how to build a EUR/CAD setup from Commitments-of-Traders data; the large-speculator (NonCommercial) group is shown net long the Euro at about 55%, a mild bullish lean, but no buy/sell price is given.

conflicts: The chart image is a Commitments-of-Traders positioning page (Long vs. Short bars), not a price chart — so the extracted entry/target prices ('value at low/high retest') do not appear anywhere on the image and cannot be trusted as levels.
The grade text itself admits the entry level is 'unclear from partial screenshot, estimated' — there is no firm number behind it.
plain_english: The only material here is one teaching screenshot showing how big speculators are positioned in the Euro versus the Canadian dollar — they lean slightly long (about 55% betting it rises). That is background positioning, not a trade: there is no buy price, no profit target, and no level that says the idea is wrong. Treat it as context only and wait — there is nothing concrete to act on until an actual price setup with real levels appears.
